channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says intel pause on Ukraine has been 'just about' lifted; says tariffs will make America rich
item.description: President Trump spoke to reporters on Air Force One on Sunday night, fielding questions about tariffs, Ukraine, Russia and retirement accounts experiencing a freefall.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its employees
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most of the 80,000 federal workers responsible for researching diseases, inspecting food and administering Medicare and Medicaid under the auspices of the Health and Human Services Department were emailed an offer to leave their job for as much as a $25,000 payment as part of Presi
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Noem taps new ICE leaders and moves to identify leakers
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem has announced new immigration enforcement leadership as she pledged to step of polygraph tests on employees to identify those who may be leaking information about immigration operations to the media.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Travel headaches show need for feds to focus on airline tech jobs as true major delay culprit revealed
item.description: Airline safety is back in the news after several tragic incidents in US skies, and airline maintenance jobs are now at a premium, experts and lawmakers said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump shifts tone on Musk as tensions rise with Cabinet
item.description: President Trump is signaling a softer approach to the mass firings of federal government workers led by Elon Musk as cracks emerge between the tech billionaire and Cabinet secretaries running the agencies he’s gutting.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans unveil bill to avoid shutdown and they're daring Democrats to oppose it
item.description: House Republicans have unveiled a 99-page spending bill that would keep federal agencies funded through Sept. 30. It would provide a slight boost to defense programs while trimming nondefense programs below 2024 budget year levels.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tribes and Native American students sue over Bureau of Indian Education firings
item.description: NORMAN, Okla. (AP) — Three tribal nations and five Native American students say in a lawsuit that the Trump administration has failed its legal obligations to tribes when it cut jobs at Bureau of Indian Education schools.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans call for Trump to cut off American Bar Association
item.description: Republican senators signed a scathing letter to the American Bar Association, calling out the organization's alleged political biases against President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Renewed debate sparks discussion on 'My Safe Florida Home' program
item.description: Governor Ron DeSantis wants more Floridians to benefit from the "My Safe Florida Home" program, which provides storm-hardening home improvements for homeowners.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A single day of Trump and Musk's cost-cutting campaign remakes huge sections of government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A series of decisions revealed Friday provided a glimpse of the turmoil engulfing federal agencies since President Donald Trump and Elon Musk launched their campaign of disruption, upending how government functions in ways big and small.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Democrats ask Rubio for answers on now-canceled $400 million Tesla plan
item.description: A letter from two House Democrats presses Rubio for details about who approved an effort to try to use hundreds of millions of dollars of taxpayer money on armored electric vehicles from Tesla.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Politics Friday: Moves in Washington hover over state budget outlook
item.description: MPR News senior politics editor Brian Bakst talked to Sen. Mark Johnson about the state’s budget situation. Plus, a conversation with the dean of Minnesota’s congressional delegation, U.S. Rep. Betty McCollum.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: State Dept using AI to ban visas over perceived pro-Hamas posts
item.description: The initiative, started under Secretary of State Marco Rubio, will use AI to scan student visa holders’ social media accounts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk tells Republican lawmakers he's not to blame for federal firings
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s not his fault. Billionaire Elon Musk is telling Republican lawmakers that he is not to blame for the firings of thousands of federal workers, including veterans, as pushes to downsize the government.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A downbeat Minnesota budget forecast initiates lawmaker blame game
item.description: Minnesota lawmakers received an economic budget forecast that shows less wiggle room as they write a new budget and an even larger looming deficit in the future. Gov. Tim Walz and DFL leaders blamed President Donald Trump while Republican leaders said Democrats need to look in the mirror.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Social Security offices in NY set to close amid DOGE budget cuts
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Several Social Security offices in New York will be closing as the Department of Government Efficiency continues to cut federal spending. The closures come after the Social Security Administration said it found over $800 million in savings for the 2025 fiscal year.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Tampa Bay Ukrainian refugees worried temporary legal status could be revoked
item.description: It has been three years since Russia invaded Ukraine sparking the latest deadly conflict between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dems should recognize issue of border security, de Blasio says
item.description: “Democrats need to say, ‘We believe in order, we believe in security, we need a border that makes sense.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Judge rules Trump illegally removed labor board member
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday ruled that President Trump unlawfully removed a Democratic member of the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB), suggesting in her ruling that the president sought to test the limits of his power in doing so. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Proposal would use cameras to ticket double parking in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- A proposed pilot program aims to crack down on various curb violations, including drivers who double park in New York City. The program would use cameras to issue automatic tickets.
item.dc:creator: Kiran Dhillon, PIX 11

item.title: US tariffs on Canada and Mexico take effect, as China takes aim at US farm exports
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico went into effect Tuesday, putting global markets on edge and setting up costly retaliations by the United States’ North American allies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration ramps up attacks as Zelenskyy thanks US
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, WFLA, NBC
